response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, GIS apps for Green Map) and has built low-latency, high-throughput backend systems in DLang handling millions of requests per second, which demonstrates systems-level thinking relevant to a routing data pipeline. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his C/C++ experience is limited and dated (National Instruments, 2014-2015, plus a thesis project), with no Rust listed. He also has no direct experience with routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile production pipelines, and his recent work has centered on TypeScript/Node full-stack and AI tooling rather than large-scale data pipeline engineering.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS platforms
Low-latency distributed systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at millions of requests/sec
Broad full-stack versatility and strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline background
Recent focus on TypeScript web/product and AI tooling, distant from systems-level pipeline work
